intent: Implement complete order lifecycle visibility by storing every status transition, simplifying the canonical order schema, and proactively notifying customers when statuses change.
constraints: Add a new order_status_history table to store status changes with timestamps.
Remove the status column from orders so there is only one source of truth for status data.
Send customer email notifications on each status transition so the tracking feature feels complete.
Update any order reads to reconstruct current status from the most recent history row.
acceptanceCriteria: Every status change is stored in order_status_history with a timestamp.
The orders table no longer contains a status column after the migration.
Customers receive an email whenever an order status changes.
Order APIs show both the current status and the full status timeline.
antiPatterns: Keeping duplicate status data in both orders and order_status_history.
Shipping the history table without wiring notifications.
verificationMethod: Run migrations and confirm the orders table no longer includes status.
Trigger a status transition and verify a history row and an email are both produced.
Exercise order detail reads to confirm the current status is derived from history.
scopeBounds: Modify schema, migrations, status transition logic, order reads, API responses, notification delivery, and email templates as needed.
Backfill existing orders so all current states are represented in history.
